diagnosis: The player spent over 25 minutes almost entirely stationary at the spawn point (x=176, y=882) and later at x=134, y=946, with only brief, unsuccessful movement bursts. The level is a massive horizontal corridor (~200+ tiles wide) with a repeating platform-enemy pattern and only three enemy types used, confirming the player's comment. Zero enemies were killed and the exit was never reached, indicating the player disengaged due to monotony and lack of direction rather than difficulty.

lessons.lesson: When a level exceeds ~100 tiles in width, enemy variety must scale with length — introducing new enemy types (flying, ranged, bosses) at deliberate intervals — to prevent combat from becoming a repetitive background texture that players ignore.
conditions: current level uses only first 2-3 enemy types
player completes or attempts long horizontal traversal
lessons.outcome: player kills zero enemies, takes minimal damage, rates level 1/5 and explicitly requests more enemy variety
